text.title: QA Flaky Test Root-Cause Taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-flaky-test-root-cause-taxonomy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Root-cause is one of exactly 8 enum values: time, order, network, concurrency, environment, randomness, external_state, infrastructure. Adding a 9th requires methodology version bump.
rule.rationale: Open enums turn into ad-hoc labels and lose stat usefulness.

rule.statement: Each category has ≥1 deterministic detector (regex on log, structural signal, or numeric threshold).
rule.rationale: Without detectors classification becomes opinion; with them it is reproducible.

rule.statement: Each category names a canonical fix family (e.g. time → freezegun + monotonic clock).
rule.rationale: Classification without remediation is bookkeeping; pairing classification with fix family closes the loop.

rule.statement: A test row receives ONE root_cause. If two are present, choose the dominant cause by detector score.
rule.rationale: Multi-cause rows make per-category KPIs meaningless.

rule.statement: If no detector fires, root_cause may be 'unknown' but only for 7 days. After that an engineer must reclassify or quarantine.
rule.rationale: Permanent 'unknown' rots; bounded unknown forces investigation.

rule.statement: Adding `pytest-rerunfailures` or `retry` is masking; it is not a category fix. Mask-based rows must declare a fix plan within 30d.
rule.rationale: Masking trades CI green for compounded technical debt; bounded mask windows force real fixes.

rule.statement: If suite is <50 tests OR org enforces another taxonomy OR all tests are UI E2E, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Generic taxonomy is wasted in those contexts.
